mnemonics.title: CPU COMPONENTS
mnemonics.content: CAUSE: Control Unit, ALU, Registers - These cause execution

mnemonics.title: INSTRUCTION CYCLE
mnemonics.content: F DES: Fetch, Decode, Execute, Store - THE BASIC STEPS

mnemonics.title: SYSTEM BUS TYPES
mnemonics.content: D A C: Data, Address, Control - THREE BUSES TO RULE

mnemonics.title: MEMORY HIERARCHY
mnemonics.content: FAST: Fastest (Registers) -> Cache -> Main Memory -> Secondary Storage -> A (slowest)

acronyms.usage: The brain of the computer that executes instructions
Performs arithmetic and logical operations in CPU
Translates virtual addresses to physical addresses
Allows I/O devices to transfer data without CPU intervention
Holds address of next instruction to execute
Communication between CPU and peripheral devices
Volatile primary memory in computer systems

visualTips.visualization: Draw CPU as a rectangle with three internal boxes: Control Unit (top), ALU (middle), Registers (bottom). Arrows show data flow between them and to/from memory.
Draw a circular diagram with four steps: Fetch -> Decode -> Execute -> Store, with arrows connecting them in sequence forming a loop.
Draw three parallel horizontal lines (buses) with CPU on left, Memory on right, and I/O devices at bottom. Show how data flows along each bus type.
Draw an inverted pyramid: Top (smallest, fastest): Registers; Next: Cache; Middle: RAM; Bottom (largest, slowest): Hard Disk. Show speed decreasing downward.
Side-by-side boxes: Von Neumann shows one memory connected to CPU with single bus; Harvard shows two separate memories each with own bus to CPU.

analogies.analogy: The CPU is like the brain of a computer - it processes information, makes decisions, and controls all other parts of the body (computer components).
CPU registers are like a scratchpad or sticky notes right next to you while working - fastest place to jot down temporary information.
Cache memory is like a desk drawer next to your main workspace - faster to access than filing cabinet (RAM) but smaller capacity.
RAM is like your main desk workspace - where active work happens, but limited space and contents lost when power off.
Hard disk is like a filing cabinet - stores everything permanently but much slower to access than your desk.

analogies.anal analogy: The system bus is like a highway connecting cities (components) - data travels on this highway between different parts of the computer.

analogies.analogy: DMA is like having a personal assistant who can deliver documents directly to colleagues without interrupting your work - frees up your time.
An interrupt is like a phone ringing during work - it pauses your current task to handle the urgent matter, then returns to work.
